behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit beats Integration only for pure status-mapping logic, but this behavior is about rename detection/representation at the CLI boundary (repo -> git diff/status -> report), so Integration beats Unit. Then System beats Integration because real git rename semantics and CLI/process I/O are the failure boundary; mocks can hide cases where git emits rename records differently or not at all. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: using real repo state plus actual CLI execution is needed to expose misclassification that in-process fixtures may miss. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit baseline, perform a rename, run the actual CLI process against that repo, parse stdout report, and assert renamed count increments while added/deleted do not include that same file. This requires real git behavior plus subprocess/stdout handling.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Integration beats Unit because the crash risk is typically at module handoff (subprocess output/decoder/parser), not a single pure function. System then beats Integration because binary diff handling depends on real git/subprocess byte output and real filesystem binary content; mocked text diffs can miss decode/stream edge cases. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both indicate System. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by lack of AI/ML output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, commit a binary file change (and optionally mixed text+binary changes), execute the CLI as a subprocess, and assert exit code is success and binary-file counts are present in stdout report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Integration beats Unit because this behavior is triggered by repository state discovery and error handling across modules, not isolated computation. System beats Integration because the key failure boundary is real git/repo initialization state and CLI/subprocess error-path behavior (e.g., git returns non-zero/fatal text for unborn HEAD). REAL-INFRA: yes, real repo/process behavior matters. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected since behavior text is deterministic infrastructure handling only.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a brand-new git repo with zero commits, run the CLI executable, and verify successful exit plus empty structured report on stdout (not traceback/error).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Integration beats Unit because this is end-to-end reconciliation across history reader, categorizer, and formatter modules. System beats Integration because correctness must be validated against real git history traversal and real CLI execution; stubs can trivially agree with themselves and hide true history-parsing mismatches. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both support System. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because no model output quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo with a scripted sequence of commits containing known adds/deletes/renames/modifications, run the CLI, and compare reported counts to independently computed ground truth from git commands/history fixtures.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Integration beats Unit because staged/unstaged equivalence depends on state handoff among working tree, index, and analyzer modules. System beats Integration since staged vs unstaged semantics are real git index/worktree behaviors and often differ only under true repo state transitions and CLI invocation. REAL-INFRA: real filesystem+git state is required; in-process mocks risk false confidence (MOCK-HIDE). Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected; no AI/ML step exists.
behaviors.test_description: Create identical content changes in two real repo scenarios (one staged, one left unstaged), run the CLI in each case, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
